key_strengths: Regulatory near-monopoly in bond ratings (NRSRO framework) blocks new entrants structurally
S&P 500 benchmark network effect: $19T+ in AUM passively linked, self-reinforcing and irreplaceable
36% FCF margin on $15.3B revenue with Ratings (64%) and Indices (69%) segment operating margins
53 consecutive years of dividend growth; 85%+ of FCF returned to shareholders annually
Asset-light model: $195M capex against $5.5B FCF — essentially all earnings are owner earnings
Four overlapping moat sources: regulatory, network effect, switching costs, data history depth
key_risks: Ratings revenue cyclicality: high-margin segment tied to debt issuance volumes, sensitive to rate cycles
AI disruption risk to Market Intelligence subscriptions over 3–7 year horizon
Price margin of safety is thin to nonexistent at $429; conservative DCFs place fair value at $369–$435
Mobility spin-off (mid-2026) introduces execution risk and expected re-rating may not fully materialize
Regulatory pressure on 'issuer pays' ratings model: low probability but high structural impact if reformed
red_flags: Bear-case DCF (GuruFocus) yields ~$251 intrinsic value — cannot be dismissed even if overly conservative
2025 FCF came in below $6B guidance, signaling modest FCF conversion pressure
GAAP EPS ($14.66) vs. adjusted EPS ($17.83) gap reflects ongoing IHS Markit amortization drag
52-week low of $381 was hit one month ago — recent recovery has eroded the brief margin of safety
IHS Markit all-stock acquisition at 2021 valuations; full value proof requires more time
buy_triggers: Stock re-tests $380–$400 range, restoring a genuine price margin of safety (~10–15% below base-case intrinsic value)
Mobility spin-off completes cleanly mid-2026, re-rating Core SPGI as a pure-play data and benchmarking business
Debt issuance volumes inflect upward on rate declines, providing multi-year Ratings revenue recovery visibility
sell_triggers: Ratings segment revenue declines for 3+ consecutive quarters due to structural (not cyclical) causes
Market Intelligence organic growth falls below 3% for two consecutive years, signaling AI-driven subscription erosion
Stock re-rates to 35x+ forward earnings without commensurate earnings acceleration — take partial profits
valuation_summary: At ~$429, SPGI trades at ~22x 2026 adjusted EPS guidance midpoint ($19.53), well below its historical 30–37x range, but conservative DCF models place intrinsic value at $369–$435, making the base case essentially in-line with the current price and leaving no meaningful margin of safety; the stock is fairly valued, not cheap.
source_summary: Analysis based on web search results from March 2026 including S&P Global Q4/FY2025 earnings release (February 10, 2026), investor relations materials, analyst consensus from MarketBeat/TipRanks, DCF estimates from Alpha Spread/Simply Wall St/GuruFocus, MacroTrends financial data, and Motley Fool earnings transcripts; no user-provided context files; training knowledge used for background on regulatory framework and historical market share.
